original_text: A chart-topping song is composed by you.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A forgettable, low-quality song that goes entirely unnoticed is composed by you.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A mediocre song that briefly gains minor local attention is composed by you.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A decent song that becomes a modest regional hit is composed by you.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A chart-topping song is composed by you.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A chart-topping song that becomes a defining anthem of its generation is composed by you.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A groundbreaking, genre-defining song that influences countless artists for decades is composed by you.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A song universally regarded as the greatest ever written, one that permanently transforms global culture and inspires humanity for centuries, is composed by you.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases in both song quality and cultural impact. Tier 1 is an unnoticed, low-quality song; Tier 4 matches the original chart-topping status; Tier 7 represents the pinnacle of musical achievement with civilization-scale cultural impact. All tiers describe the same core scenario—you composing a song—with only the quality and cultural reach varying.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a rational agent would prefer composing a song of the highest possible quality with the deepest and most enduring cultural impact over one with lesser quality or reach.
